Part B.key_evidence: Single detection (ndethist=1, deltajd=0) of a point-like source (classtar=0.983, fwhm=1.86 px) with positive subtraction at r=18.93. Nearest PS1 source is 10.17 arcsec away (sgscore1=0.997, a star) — unrelated to the candidate. No obvious host galaxy is visible at the candidate location in the reference cutout. ncovhist=1107 indicates the field has been observed many times yet only one detection exists here.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: A single-epoch, point-like, isolated transient with no catalogued counterpart and no nearby host most likely represents a solar-system object (asteroid) moving through the field. The lack of any PS1 object at the candidate position, the high classtar, and only one detection in a heavily covered field all support a transient point source rather than repeated stellar/AGN variability.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A supernova is plausible if the host galaxy is too faint to appear in the reference, but no host is evident in the cutouts and the PS1 neighbor at 10" is a star, weakening that option. Variable star/AGN are disfavored because repeated variability would likely yield more detections given ncovhist=1107 and there is no underlying PS1 source at the position.
